message.content:
The user wants me to create webpage 2 (site/page-2.html) and link it back to page 1 (site/index.html) without breaking page 1. Looking at the existing files: - site/index.html exists with a nav element that's currently empty For this stage (web-002-backlink), I need to: 1. Create site/page-2.html 2. Add a link from page-2.html back to index.html 3. Possibly update index.html to include a link to page-2.html (to make it a proper chain) The validators that will run: - page-2-links-index: failure_class=link_integrity - This means page-2 must link to index - all-html-parse: failure_class=syntax - Both HTML files must be valid Let me create page-2.html with a link back to index.html, and I should also update index.html to link to page-2.html to create a proper chain. Actually, looking at the stage name "backlink", it seems like the main goal is to link back TO page 1 FROM page 2. But to not break page 1, I should also add a link from page 1 to page 2 so users can navigate both ways. Let me create: 1. site/page-2.html - with content and a link back to index.html 2. Update site/index.html - add a link to page-2.html in the nav Both files need to be valid HTML. { "files": [ { "path": "site/page-2.html", "content": "\n\n\n \n \n Page 2\n\n\n
\n Home\n
\n
\n

Page 2

\n

This is the second page.

\n
\n\n" }, { "path": "site/index.html", "content": "\n\n\n \n \n My Website\n\n\n
\n Page 2\n
\n
\n

Welcome

\n

This is the main content of the page.

\n
\n\n" } ], "notes": "Created page-2.html with link back to index.html, and updated index.html nav to link to page-2." }
